rationale: All eight events are Security Event 5145 (network share object access) generated by the machine account 'user-01$' from a link-local IPv6 address, consistent with a domain-joined computer performing routine Group Policy refresh. The IPC$/lsarpc accesses (A0, EV-004–EV-007) represent standard MSRPC/LSARPC named-pipe connections used during GP processing, while the SYSVOL accesses (EV-001–EV-003) show the machine reading the default domain policy and default domain controllers policy gpt.ini files — exactly the files a Windows client enumerates during a normal GP update cycle. The access masks (0x12019f for IPC$, 0x100081/0x120089 for SYSVOL) are read-oriented and consistent with legitimate GP enumeration; no write, create, or delete access is indicated. No anomalous source process, lateral movement indicators, or access to sensitive shares (e.g., ADMIN$, C$) are present, and the actor is a computer account rather than an interactive user, further supporting routine automated behavior.
